title: astragalus nuttallianus var. trichocarpus
intro: Astragalus nuttallianus var. trichocarpus, also known as the White Lomatium or White Biscuitroot, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is primarily known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant's roots and stems have been traditionally used in herbal preparations to address respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to support the immune system and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of Astragalus nuttallianus var. trichocarpus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its potential health benefits.
